invalidation.condition: Enterprise security budget growth turns negative OR a bellwether platform (CrowdStrike / Palo Alto / Zscaler) guides revenue DOWN materially for 2 consecutive quarters.
invalidation.eval_note: NOT machine-checkable — requires reading platform earnings guidance. Claude-checks-with-WebSearch. This is the structural-deactivation stop for struct_enterprise_cyber_spend_supercycle: cyber spend is famously sticky/non-discretionary, so a sustained guide-down is the cleanest signal the supercycle is breaking.
invalidation.rationale: The position rests on enterprise cyber spend being a secular, recession-resistant budget line. Two consecutive down-guides at the platform leaders would falsify that. Full exit — the thesis is dead, not just timing.

invalidation.condition: USPY.L closes more than 20% below entry price for 10 consecutive trading days.
invalidation.eval_note: Machine-checkable: USPY.L is the traded vehicle (USD-quoted accumulating line of the L&G Cyber Security ETF — the one actually held). Threshold 32.74 USD = entry 40.92 USD − 20%, in the same USD units yfinance returns (no pence/×100 issue — that was the ISPY.L GBp line, now corrected).
invalidation.rationale: A 20%/10d drawdown triggers a REVIEW, not a reflex exit — given the overbought entry, a near-term pullback is likely and is not itself a thesis break. Exit only if paired with inv_01 firing.

invalidation.condition: Cybersecurity's 90-day return correlation to the held AI-capex cluster (copper + grid + semis) rises above 0.50 for two consecutive monthly reviews.
invalidation.eval_note: Computed via dislocation --anchor-sectors (90d yfinance correlation). NOT a price stop — it tests the DIVERSIFICATION thesis directly: if cyber starts moving WITH the AI cluster, the reason for holding it (decorrelation) has evaporated.
invalidation.rationale: This position exists to diversify the AI-capex concentration. If its correlation to that cluster rises above 0.5, it is no longer doing that job → reduce and redeploy the diversification budget elsewhere. This is the experiment's specific kill-switch.

assumptions.statement: Enterprise cybersecurity spending keeps growing double-digit YoY during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: Gartner/IDC security-spend forecasts + platform-leader revenue. Falsified by: sector revenue growth decelerates below ~5% YoY for 2 consecutive quarters.
assumptions.status_note: Cyber spend is structurally sticky (non-discretionary, regulation- and threat-driven). struct_enterprise_cyber_spend_supercycle intensity 86 at entry.

assumptions.statement: Cybersecurity stays low-correlation (<0.30, 90d) to the AI-capex cluster the book is already long.
assumptions.measurability: Source: dislocation correlation engine (90d yfinance). Falsified by: corr-to-book > 0.30 sustained (and the inv_03 kill-switch at 0.50).
assumptions.status_note: Corr-to-book 0.06 at entry — the lowest of any rotation target. This LOW correlation is the entire reason for the position.

assumptions.measurability: Source: intensity_engine + narrative_maturity. Falsified by: intensity < 70 for 2 consecutive reviews OR narrative reaches 'exhausted'.

risk_discipline.note: Diversification rotation, NOT a new correlated bet. struct_enterprise_cyber_spend_supercycle had €0 prior exposure, so this does NOT breach the 20% correlated-catalyst cap — it is the deliberate counterweight to the ~82% AI-capex cluster concentration (copper/grid/semis). Chosen because cybersecurity_commercial is the #1 rotation target: composite 83.8, regime intact, and corr 0.06 to the existing book (the lowest available). ACKNOWLEDGED: entry timing is overbought (RSI 68.9, 87.7% of 52w range, +volume surge) → entry_timing said wait_stabilize; see sizing_decision. Decision Journal experiment #2.

metadata.attribution_note: Single driver: struct_enterprise_cyber_spend_supercycle at 1.0 (the sector's only active catalyst).
metadata.fill_note: RECONCILED with the actual broker fill (2026-06-08): the vehicle held is USPY.L — the USD-quoted ACCUMULATING line of the L&G Cyber Security UCITS ETF — not the GBp-quoted ISPY.L originally drafted (equivalent index, cleaner currency). Fill: qty 13.99 @ 40.92 USD close, amount_eur=500 gross (avg cost ≈ €35.74/unit at EURUSD ≈ 1.1547; the ~€0.30/unit over the €35.44 market price is the fee/spread wedge baked into amount_eur). USD→EUR FX handled in mark-to-market + nav_engine; the earlier GBp/×100 hazard no longer applies.

experiment.hypothesis: Cybersecurity (enterprise cyber-spend supercycle) is a genuine, durable DIVERSIFIER to the AI-capex cluster: adding it lowers book correlation/drawdown risk without sacrificing return, because cyber spend is secular and largely decoupled from the AI-capex cycle.
experiment.what_would_disprove: inv_03 fires — cyber's 90d correlation to the AI-cluster book rises above 0.50 (the decorrelation evaporates) — OR struct_enterprise_cyber_spend_supercycle weakens (inv_01 / asm_03). A drawdown that stays UNCORRELATED to the book does NOT disprove it.
accepted_risks: overbought entry (RSI 68.9, 87.7% of 52w range, volume surge ×5.53 — chasing strength)
entry_timing said wait_stabilize; entered anyway (see sizing_decision)
USD-quoted vehicle (USPY.L) → USD/EUR FX on the mark
diversification thesis could decay if a macro shock correlates everything

entry_technicals.source: technical_study.py (deep TA, opt-in via catalyx-open step 5.6)
bullish: MACD above signal and above zero
cautions: volume surge ×5.53 vs 20d avg (move has participation)

sizing_decision.ta_recommendation: wait_stabilize / scale_in — overbought, near 52w highs; ideally wait for a pullback toward the band midline
sizing_decision.rationale: Override of the timing caution: the VALUE here is the decorrelation (0.06), not the entry tick. The AI-capex cluster carries ~82% concentration RIGHT NOW, so the diversification benefit is wanted now; waiting for a cyber pullback leaves the book concentrated in the interim. Satellite-sized (€500), bounded by inv_02 (−20%/10d review). Logged so the close can judge full-size-now vs the scale-in the TA preferred.
